name: Gingerbread Teddy Bears
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine butter, brown sugar, and corn syrup. Cook and stir over medium heat until the sugar dissolves.
2. Pour the mixture into a large mixing bowl and let it cool for about 5 minutes.
3. Sift together flour, cinnamon, ginger, cloves, and baking soda.
4. Add egg and vanilla to the butter mixture; mix well.
5. Gradually add the sifted dry ingredients to the wet mixture; beat until well combined.
6. Divide the dough in half. Cover and chill for 2 hours or overnight.
7. For each bear:
- Shape the dough into one 1-inch ball, one 3/4-inch ball, six 1/2-inch balls, and one 1/4-inch ball.
- Flatten the 1-inch ball to about 1/2 inch thickness for the body on an ungreased cookie sheet.
- Attach the 3/4-inch ball for the head and flatten it to about 1/2 inch thickness.
- Attach the 1/2-inch balls for arms, legs, and ears.
- Attach the 1/4-inch ball for the nose.
8. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 8 to 10 minutes. Let cool completely before decorating.
9. Decorate with icing as desired. For Easter, use light corn syrup instead of molasses, substitute white sugar for brown sugar, omit spices, and add a bit of almond extract if desired. Create two long ears on top and two cheek balls instead of the nose ball.
(Note: The original recipe text has been retained in its entirety.)
